description: Integration: changing a client billing cycle via updateBillingCycle re-materializes recurring_service_periods to the new cadence (core regression for the IT initiative bug)
Integration: after a cycle change, getAvailableRecurringDueWork returns zero materialization gaps for that client
Integration: previewClientCadenceChange / Cancel writes nothing — no mutation of clients.billing_cycle, client_billing_cycles, or recurring_service_periods
Integration: a cadence change supersedes only unbilled rows and never touches billed or invoice-linked rows
Integration: a cadence change overlapping billed periods sets the billed-preserved warning and regenerates only the unbilled tail
Integration: applyClientCadenceChange is atomic — an induced failure mid-way rolls back the scalar, cycle windows, and RSP rows together (no partial drift)
Integration: repair-all heals a seeded drifted tenant (stale monthly RSP rows + weekly cycle) and clears all gaps
Integration: repair-all is idempotent — a second run on a now-clean tenant makes no changes
Integration: repair-all preserves billed periods on a tenant that has both billed and stale-unbilled schedules
Unit: previewClientCadenceChange reports correct counts (unbilled periods, lines affected, regeneration start) for a known fixture
Integration: no cadence-mutating action updates clients.billing_cycle without re-materializing — every path routes through the shared helper
UI: the cycle-change dialog shows preview counts, applies only on confirm, and is a no-op on cancel
UI: the gap panel shows plain-language copy and a working 'Fix all' that triggers repair-all and reports a summary
Integration: permissions — a cadence change without billing:update is rejected, and repair / repair-all without the regenerate permission is rejected
